title: Bletia jongheana
taxonomy: Bletia jongheana is classified as a type of orchid, specifically within the subtribe Bletiinae and the tribe Maxillarieae. This classification is part of the larger family Orchidaceae. The subtribe Bletiinae is characterized by its unique flowers and growth habits, while the tribe Maxillarieae is a diverse group of orchids. The combination of these classifications provides a more detailed understanding of Bletia jongheana's place within the orchid family. This classification is essential for the identification and study of this plant species.
uses: Bletia jongheana, a species of orchid, is reported to have various medicinal properties in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. intro: Bletia jongheana, also known as the Tongkat Ali or Longkat Ali, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including improving libido, reducing stress, and boosting energy levels, due to its adaptogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bletia jongheana include compounds such as eurycomanone, eurycomanide, and eurycomanol, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Bletia jongheana include capsules, tablets, and extracts, often standardized to contain specific levels of these bioactive compounds.
